title: Generative AI Developer (Fresher)
company_focus:
Digital healthcare solutions including EHR, billing,
payments, and revenue cycle management
description:
Software Engineer – Generative AI (Fresher)

Create the Future of e-Health Together With Us

At CompuGroup Medical (CGM), our mission is to build
groundbreaking solutions for digital healthcare. We
revolutionize how healthcare professionals create, access,
and use information—so they can focus on what matters most:
patient outcomes.

As one of the Best in KLAS organizations, CGM delivers a
full suite of Electronic Health Records (EHR), Billing,
Payments, and Revenue Cycle Management (RCM) solutions. We
empower our employees with continuous learning, hands-on
experience, and opportunities to grow across modern
technologies.

What you can Expect from us:

- High-Performing Team: Work with a close-knit, fast-moving,
  and collaborative team.
- In-Person Work Culture: Onsite role based in Noida, enabling
  real collaboration and long-term professional
  relationships.
- Comprehensive Benefits: Group health and accidental
  insurance coverage.
- Seamless Onboarding: Structured onboarding and digital
  application process.
- Engaging Work Environment: Fun activities, team events, and
  games.
- Career Growth: Clear learning paths, mentorship, and merit-
  based increments.
- Job Security: A stable, recession-resilient organization.
- Flexible Transportation: Pick-up/drop-off service or
  transport allowance.
- Subsidized Meals: Affordable meals through our kitchen and
  food hall.

Job Details:

- Internship Duration: 3 months (with Stipend)
- Conversion Opportunity: Basis on performance during
  internship, we can offer a full-time role, with a
  compensation upto INR 5LPA along with travel allowance.
- Location: Sector- 58, Noida
- Start Date: Immediately
- Shift Timing: 12:00PM-9:00PM
- Work Schedule: 5 days a week (WFO)

Key Responsibilities:

- Write clean, efficient, and well-documented code with
  guidance from senior engineers.
- Assist in developing AI-driven applications using Generative
  AI and LLMs.
- Experiment with prompt engineering and Retrieval-Augmented
  Generation (RAG) techniques.
- Support fine-tuning and evaluation of pre-trained AI models.
- Help integrate AI models into backend services and APIs.
- Work on Agentic AI workflows to automate tasks and
  processes.
- Monitor and support AI services in staging and production
  environments.
- Collaborate with product, engineering, and QA teams to
  deliver quality solutions.
- Stay updated with the latest advancements in Generative AI
  and healthcare technology.
- Take ownership of assigned tasks and contribute to team
  success.

You are a good fit if you

- Hold a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, AI/ML, Data
  Science, or a related field.
- Have basic knowledge of Python and software development
  fundamentals.
- Understand core concepts of LLMs, NLP, or Machine Learning.
- Have hands-on exposure (academic projects, internships,
  self-learning) to:
  - Prompt engineering
  - APIs (REST basics)
  - RAG or vector databases (basic level)
- Are curious, proactive, and eager to learn new tools and
  technologies.
- Have good problem-solving and communication skills.

Additional Requirement (Not Mandatory):

- Internship or project experience in AI/ML or Generative AI
- Familiarity with tools like OpenAI, Gemini, Claude,
  LangChain, or similar frameworks

How to Apply

If you are passionate about AI, software engineering, and
healthcare innovation, we encourage you to apply—even if you
don’t meet every requirement.

We create the future of e-Health. Become part of a
meaningful mission.

ai_classification_reasoning:
This is an entry-level role focused on developing AI-driven
applications using Generative AI and LLMs. The
responsibilities include hands-on work with prompt
engineering, RAG implementation, fine-tuning models,
building agentic AI workflows, and integrating AI models
into production systems.
